newsSinceJun6: Jun 9 NZX market update 'Scott Technology Expands MHL Globally': over NZ$12m of new production-zone contracts in Europe and North America - central pallet conveyors for Dutch vegetable processor Bakker, a multiline palletising installation at a Canadian frozen-potato facility, and four palletising systems plus four NexBot AGVs for a JBS poultry plant in Georgia, US. Materials handling & logistics revenue was +21% to $65m in H1 FY26; Destination 2030 target of $530m revenue reiterated. Today's +6.2% move maps directly to this announcement, i.e. fundamental rather than flow-driven. Caveat: JBS is also Scott's majority shareholder, so part of the order flow is related-party.

sources.label: Farmers Weekly - Scott Tech rolls out more overseas contracts (Jun 9, 2026)
NZX SCT announcements - 'Scott Technology Expands MHL Globally' 09/06/2026
Investing.com - Scott Technology HY26 slides: diversification drives 7% EBITDA growth

verdicts.rankRationale: Genuinely new fundamental evidence since yesterday's hold: $12m+ of fresh palletising/AGV wins announced today, including NexBot autonomous robots, directly validating the physical-AI/robotics scaling thesis on a $120M, under-owned NZ name still down ~11% YTD - today's +6.2% pop consumes little of the residual alpha.
verdicts.bull: MHL division growing 21% with multi-region wins (Bakker, Canada, JBS US) and NexBot AGV deployments show the food-automation structural shift is accelerating; forward book of $177m and the $530m FY30 target imply revenue could roughly double with the stock still below where it started the year.
verdicts.neutral: $12m of contracts is small against a ~$250m revenue run-rate, and a 6% pop on a NZ$0.2b illiquid name can mean-revert; H1 showed only 5% revenue and 7% EBITDA growth, so this remains a steady compounder rather than a hyper-scaler.
verdicts.bear: JBS owns a majority stake and is also a marquee customer, capping the free float, governance optionality and takeout premium; the May CFO resignation and Dunedin WorkSafe matter add execution noise, and food/meat-processing capex is cyclical.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Further MHL/NexBot contract announcements and FY26 full-year results showing the anticipated stronger second half against the $177m forward book.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Order lumpiness or a protein/food capex slowdown stalls the Destination 2030 ramp while the JBS-controlled register keeps the stock illiquid and discounted.
